name: Bao Zheng
description: Bao Zheng, also known as Justice Bao, is a revered figure in Chinese folklore and popular religion, often elevated to the status of a deity. He is not a deity in the traditional sense of a creator god or natural force, but rather a deified historical figure celebrated for his unwavering integrity, incorruptibility, and dedication to justice. During the Song dynasty, Bao Zheng served as a government official and judge, earning a reputation for his impartiality and his willingness to challenge corruption, even at the highest levels of government. His legendary exploits, often embellished in operas and stories, depict him as a champion of the oppressed, capable of discerning truth and delivering fair verdicts where others had failed. He is a symbol of moral rectitude and the embodiment of justice in the face of injustice, inspiring generations with his unwavering commitment to upholding the law and protecting the innocent. His dark complexion and crescent-shaped birthmark are iconic features often depicted in his iconography. Temples and shrines dedicated to Bao Zheng are found throughout China, where he is venerated as a protector and a bringer of justice.
subtitle: Lord of Justice

keyMyths.title: The Case of the Chenzhou Grain
keyMyths.description: Bao Zheng is tasked with investigating corruption in the distribution of grain in Chenzhou. He uncovers a complex web of embezzlement and bribery implicating high-ranking officials. Despite facing immense pressure and threats, Bao Zheng meticulously gathers evidence, exposes the culprits, and ensures the stolen grain is returned to the people, demonstrating his unwavering commitment to justice even when confronted by powerful adversaries. This case highlights his courage and incorruptibility.
keyMyths.source: Various Peking Opera scripts and folk tales

keyMyths.title: The Case of the Civet Cat Prince
keyMyths.description: A palace intrigue revolves around the substitution of a civet cat for the emperor's newborn son. Years later, Bao Zheng investigates the strange circumstances surrounding the current emperor's birth. Through careful investigation and deduction, he uncovers the truth, revealing the conspiracy and restoring the rightful heir to the throne. This myth showcases Bao Zheng's keen intellect and his ability to unravel complex mysteries to achieve justice.
keyMyths.source: Dramas and oral tradition, often attributed to historical anecdotes

keyMyths.title: The Case of Killing Chen Shimei
keyMyths.description: Chen Shimei abandons his wife and children after achieving success in the imperial examinations and marries a princess. His abandoned wife eventually finds him and seeks justice. Bao Zheng, despite the potential political ramifications of executing a royal consort's husband, upholds the law and orders Chen Shimei's execution. This tale emphasizes Bao Zheng's impartiality and his willingness to prioritize justice above personal safety and political expediency, making him a symbol of righteousness.
keyMyths.source: Yuan Dynasty Zaju opera and subsequent adaptations

sources.author: Idema, Wilt L.
sources.work: Personal Salvation and Filial Piety: Two Precious Scroll Narratives of Guanyin and Her Acolytes
sources.description: Examines the religious and cultural context of figures like Bao Zheng within Chinese popular religion.

sources.author: Hayden, George A.
sources.work: Crime and Punishment in Medieval Chinese Drama: Three Judge Pao Plays
sources.description: Analyzes the portrayal of Bao Zheng in Yuan Dynasty dramas, focusing on themes of justice and morality.
sources.text: Analyzes the portrayal of Bao Zheng in Yuan Dynasty dramas, focusing on themes of justice and morality.
